column1: # critical_auths.tsv  -  critical authorization grants, co-owned by /sap-explain-role
# and /sap-suim (one shared vocabulary, two renderers). Customer override wins
# NEVER the LLM), evaluated against decoded AGR_1251 rows (OBJECT, FIELD, LOW, HIGH):
#   * a rule matches an object's grant when auth_object matches AND
#       - field is blank                          -> object presence alone flags, OR
#       - the granted (LOW..HIGH) range COVERS the rule's low..high value:
#           granted LOW '*'                        -> grant covers everything -> match (any rule)
#           rule low '*'                           -> flags ONLY a wildcard grant (granted LOW='*');
#                                                     to flag ANY grant of an object, leave field BLANK
#           rule low a trailing-'*' pattern        -> SAP prefix match against granted LOW
#           rule low..high interval + granted LOW  -> containment (granted value in [low,high])
#           exact                                  -> granted LOW == rule low (HIGH honored)
# severity maps onto sap_finding_lib: CRITICAL->BLOCKER, HIGH->HIGH, MEDIUM->MEDIUM.

column7: rationale
Change access to table maintenance (SM30/SM31) - can alter customizing/master data outside apps
Table maintenance across ALL authorization groups (no table-group restriction)
Debug-and-replace (change field values at runtime) - bypasses all application controls
Create/modify repository objects (ABAP Workbench write) on this system
Run arbitrary ABAP reports (ABAP editor) - broad program execution
Run arbitrary ABAP reports (report execution)
Generic table data browser - read any table's data
Generic table data browser (SE16N) - read/edit any table's data
Generic table maintenance dialog
Execute external OS commands from SAP
Maintain external OS commands
User administration (create users / reset passwords)
Role maintenance (grant oneself or others any authorization)
Client administration (change client-change settings)
Wildcard transaction-code grant - can start ANY transaction
Call ANY RFC-enabled function module (broad remote execution surface)
Write to arbitrary OS files from ABAP (application-server filesystem)
Maintain users in any user group (assign roles / unlock / reset)
Assign roles to users (grant authorizations to others)
Create/administer transports (move code/config between systems)
Batch administration (manage all background jobs)
System spool administration
